name: sop-code-review
description: Comprehensive code review workflow coordinating quality, security, performance, and documentation reviewers. 4-hour timeline for thorough multi-agent review.
capabilities: Runs automated checks (linting, tests, coverage, build validation)
Spawns parallel specialized reviewers (quality, security, performance, architecture, docs)
Aggregates findings and categorizes by severity
Generates final review summary with approval recommendations
Notifies PR authors with actionable feedback
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only workflow instructions and prompt templates. No executable code, scripts, file system access, network calls, or command execution capabilities. The static findings are all false positives: JavaScript template literals were misidentified as shell backticks, metadata fields were flagged as hardcoded URLs, and documentation text was misclassified as cryptographic algorithms.
valueStatement: Manual code reviews are time-consuming and inconsistent. This workflow coordinates multiple specialized AI agents to perform thorough reviews across quality, security, performance, architecture, and documentation in parallel.
limitations: Requires MCP servers for agent orchestration (ruv-swarm)
Cannot directly modify code or merge pull requests
Reviews are limited to code patterns, not runtime behavior
Depends on existing test suites for coverage analysis

useCases.title: PR code review
useCases.description: Automate comprehensive reviews for pull requests before merge

useCases.title: Security vulnerability scan
useCases.description: Identify OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities and security issues in code

useCases.title: Architecture assessment
useCases.description: Evaluate code changes for design consistency and scalability

promptTemplates.title: Quick PR review
promptTemplates.prompt: Run sop-code-review on PR #123 in repository my-org/backend-api. Focus on security and performance.

promptTemplates.title: Security audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute the security review phase of sop-code-review for the authentication module. Check for OWASP vulnerabilities.

promptTemplates.title: Full integration review
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the full sop-code-review workflow on PR #456. Include integration testing and deployment impact assessment.

promptTemplates.title: Architecture review
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the architecture review phase for the microservices refactoring PR. Assess scalability and dependency patterns.

bestPractices: Run automated checks first to filter obviously broken PRs before manual review
Use parallel specialized reviewers to complete comprehensive review within 4 hours
Categorize findings by severity (blocking, high, medium, low) for clear action items
antiPatterns: Skipping automated checks and going straight to manual review
Reviewing only one aspect (like security) without quality or performance checks
Merging without addressing blocking issues identified in the review

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Requires ruv-swarm MCP server for agent orchestration.

faq.question: How long does a full review take?
faq.answer: The complete workflow runs approximately 4 hours with automated checks in 30 minutes and specialized reviews in 2 hours.

faq.question: Can I run only specific review phases?
faq.answer: Yes, individual phases can be invoked separately. The workflow supports running security-only or performance-only reviews.

faq.question: Is my code data sent externally?
faq.answer: No code leaves your environment. All processing happens through local AI tools and MCP servers you control.

faq.question: What if automated checks fail?
faq.answer: The workflow stops and requests fixes from the PR author before proceeding with manual review phases.

faq.question: How is this different from single-agent review?
faq.answer: Multiple specialized agents review different aspects simultaneously, providing deeper coverage than a single generalist agent.
